intro: Migrating from JavaScript to C# transforms a dynamic scripting stack into a structured, component-driven enterprise architecture hosted on the .NET runtime core. This upgrade provides developers with deep static safety guarantees, robust enterprise development pipelines, and multi-threaded processing mechanics. The shift requires moving from open prototype chaining models to rigorous nominal class structures.

diffs.from: Dynamic types resolved at runtime execution boundaries, permitting implicit field creations.
diffs.to: Static, nominal typing architecture enforced at compilation, supporting clean explicit interfaces and generic abstractions.

diffs.from: Lenient syntax rules supporting functional expressions, isolated code variables, and loosely ordered executions.
diffs.to: Strict block-scoped layout requiring code encapsulation inside defined namespaces, classes, and formal visibility scopes.

diffs.from: Dynamic runtime engine memory garbage collector optimized for UI interface adjustments and DOM changes.
diffs.to: High-performance generational garbage collector built directly into the .NET runtime framework.

diffs.from: Single-threaded event loop engine triggering microtask queues and asynchronous callback workflows.
diffs.to: Task-based Asynchronous Pattern (TAP) using async/await syntax to distribute concurrent workloads across multi-core CPU threads.

diffs.from: Focused on file/network IO primitives, browser DOM structures, and basic variable data utilities.
diffs.to: Comprehensive .NET class ecosystem providing advanced LINQ data tools, native cryptography, and optimized data stream structures.

examples.title: Data Query and Processing
examples.description: Translating array functional maps into clean, structured .NET LINQ statements.

examples.title: Asynchronous Task Definition
examples.description: Converting a standard JavaScript Promise routine into an idiomatic C# Task execution pattern.

tips.title: Leverage LINQ Transformations
tips.body: Replace iterative for loops or complex map chains with C#'s highly expressive LINQ expression keywords.

tips.title: Enforce Proper Properties
tips.body: Avoid raw public fields. Use implicit property accessors (public string Name { get; set; }) to preserve proper encapsulation patterns.

tips.title: Handle Nullable Contexts
tips.body: Enable modern C# nullable context settings (#nullable enable) to track potential null assignment risks at compilation time.

tips.title: Convert Structs explicitly
tips.body: Evaluate whether your lightweight data payloads fit C# 'struct' patterns rather than always relying on heavy heap-allocated classes.

faqs.question: Can I use dynamic fields in C#?
faqs.answer: Yes, C# includes the 'dynamic' keyword which bypasses static compile-time checking, but it should be used sparingly as it reduces type safety and performance.

faqs.question: What is the equivalent of JavaScript arrays in C#?
faqs.answer: Use List<T> for dynamic resizing arrays, or T[] for fixed-length memory allocations.

faqs.question: How do modules translate?
faqs.answer: JavaScript ES modules map directly to C# files wrapped in explicit namespace scopes, organized globally via assemblies and projects.
